mnemonics.title: VIEW Types记忆
mnemonics.content:
SIMPLE: Single table, No aggregates, Updatable
COMPLEX: Multiple tables, Has aggregates, May not update
READ ONLY: Cannot modify data
CHECK OPTION: Validates data before insert/update

acronyms.usage: Operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) that can be performed on updatable views
Commands like CREATE, ALTER, DROP used for view definitions
Language used to create and query views

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box representing the view with arrows pointing to base tables. Show query going into view box and data flowing out as if from a table.
Create a flowchart: Single table + No aggregates = Updatable path. Multiple tables/Aggregates = Non-updatable path.
Show a filter icon on view where INSERT/UPDATE must pass through. Data not satisfying WHERE clause gets rejected.

analogies.analogy: Like a car dashboard - shows specific information (speed, fuel) from complex systems underneath, without exposing everything.
Like a printed photograph vs live video stream - the photo (materialized) stores a fixed moment, video (regular view) shows current data.
Like privacy screen on phone - limits what others can see while the actual screen (base table) remains unchanged.
